response.justification: Candidate has 13+ years of software engineering experience and some adjacent developer-tooling work (MCP generators, CI/CD setup, GitLab admin, internal test runner authorship). However, the resume shows no formal people-management role, no Engineering Manager title, and no evidence of leading organizational change or running 1:1s/performance cycles. Mobile platform exposure is thin (brief iOS/Apple TV maintenance years ago). Strong individual-contributor and craftsmanship signal, but a clear gap against the core people-leadership requirement of this role.
strengths: 13+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Hands-on developer-tooling work: MCP code generators, CI servers, GitLab/Docker/Artifactory, custom test runner (Trial)
Demonstrated mentorship via workshops, Selenium course, refactoring dojos, and conference talks
concerns: No explicit people-management experience or direct reports listed; missing the required 2 years of people leadership
No mobile-platform ecosystem depth (brief iOS work in 2015–2016, nothing on Android/large mobile build systems)
No evidence of leading organizational change or owning team roadmaps at manager scope
